'''Object 36'''
Slime Organ
'''SAFETY'''
Safe if Cooked
'''RARITY'''
Common
'''BIOHAZARD'''
Low
'''AREAS:'''
Forest and indoor, abandoned levels.

== Description == {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Slime Organ.jpg|caption=A piece of orange '''Slime Organ''' found on [[Level 158]].}} '''Object 36''', also known as "Slime Organ" (''singular and plural '''Slime Organ'''''), is a relatively common object in [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]]. It is a slime-like, gelatinous, organic substance of varying colors that grows on corners of indoor levels. '''Slime Organ''' take their name from their slimy composition and organ-like appearance and functioning. Despite being very similar to the ordinary slime, '''Slime Organ''' detains several noticeable differences to an average slime. Among these differences are: Slime Organ are more watery than the average slime; Slime Organ contain advanced and complicated systems very different from a slime; they are much more resistant; and they are fully organic and edible. There are a number of variations of '''Slime Organ''', from a '''Slime Organ''' that is simply blue to a neon '''Slime Organ''' to a cotton candy-smelling '''Slime Organ'''. The interior of a '''Slime Organ''''s body is fairly complicated. The entire object is filled with a liquid that feeds the '''Slime Organ''', helps it grow, and assists in generating new cells. This liquid is essential for the '''Slime Organ''' and it will quickly rot away if it is not present. Strangely enough, heating a '''Slime Organ''' to temperatures higher than 40° Celsius or putting a lot of pressure into it will cause the cells to begin an emergency operation; the cells will begin modifying the '''Slime Organ''''s inner liquid and turning it into the same outer substance of the '''Slime Organ'''. This makes eating an undecooked or raw '''Slime Organ''' an extremely hazardous act, as the '''Slime Organ''' may become stuck to the wanderer's throat and begin growing and feeding on the wanderer's meat. {{Blurimage|align=right|file=honeything.webp|caption=Slime Organ fluid stored in a honey bottle.|hovertext=}} '''Slime Organ''' contains a strange property that causes it to extremely quickly regenerate once it is cut in half. This is caused by the excessive amount of cells and proteins within '''Slime Organ'''; these cells are quickly deployed to the injury and quickly regenerate it. Due to this, some believe '''Slime Organ''' are completely solid. No matter how fast a wanderer or a machine attempts to cut the '''Slime Organ''', the only way for one to see the inner liquid is to collect it using a serynge. One would have to remove one of the organ-like structures within '''Slime Organ''' for the regeration process to cease. Inside '''Slime Organ''', there are several organ-like structures, denoted "glossums," inspired by the person who discovered them. These organ-like structures are essential for the '''Slime Organ''''s existence and growing cycle, and a '''Slime Organ''' without even just one of these structures would collapse. There are four glossums inside of a '''Slime Organ'''; the Cellar, responsible for generating cells of most types; the Reloses, responsible for reproduction; the Takers, outer glossums that collect nutrients and food from outside and provide them to the '''Slime Organ'''; and the outer substance covering the entirety of the '''Slime Organ''', and preventing the liquid from leaking and glossums from getting out, named after the same object. Some specimens of '''Slime Organ''' contain "bulbs." Bulbs are inflamated areas within the '''Slime Organ''' that are filled with bacteria and viruses and contain no liquid within them. Bulbs are caused by the cells of the '''Slime Organ''' itself, who redirect all of the hazardous bacteria to a specific area, deemed useless by them, and the bacteria become stuck into the area. Before cooking a '''Slime Organ''', one should immediately search for bulbs, as the bacteria are freed from the bulb when the '''Slime Organ''' is cooked, and they will spread around the object. The best way to remove a bulb is by using tools similar to scissors to cut them off. The danger of a bulb depends on its age; older bulbs contain more bacteria than newer ones. Eating a '''Slime Organ''' with a bulb, independently of age, is extremely hazardous for a human, as various viruses and bacteria will enter one's body. '''Slime Organ''' can easily catch dust, hair, and other pieces of dirt on it that are quite difficult to remove due to their size. These pieces of dirt may even be absorbed into the '''Slime''' '''Organ''', causing the particles to be spread around the object and making the process of removing them much, much more difficult. Therefore, a '''Slime Organ''' should be carefully washed and inspected, and any pieces of dirt must be carefully taken out using tools before cooking and consumption. If one eats '''Slime Organ''' with pieces of dirt, other than causing diseases, the pieces may block outer heat from the '''Slime Organ''', causing some liquid to remain, and the '''Slime Organ''' may continue growing inside of the wanderer. When compared side by side, one can notice several differences between a natural '''Slime Organ''' and an industrialized one, specifically physical. A natural '''Slime Organ''' is generally more messy, with strange patterns and deformities that cause it to appear as a more gelatinous rock. The natural '''Slime Organ''' also contains several dirt pieces. Meanwhile, the industrialized '''Slime Organ''' is smooth and flat; it is clean and cooked and is only encountered inside containers. == Usage == {{Blurimage|align=right|file=Slime Patches.jpg|caption=A tray of '''Slime Patches'''.}} '''Slime Organ''' are commonly used for consumption due to being tasty, healthy, and easy to encounter, causing them to be a popular and handy snack all across [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]], being among the most popular foods in the entirety of it. Although it can also be used for protection, due to their resistance and being very elastic, they are able to support being stretched to a one-meter length before being torn apart. === Cooking and Consumption === There are several ways one can cook and create '''Slime Organ''' dishes; there are hundreds of dishes using them; therefore, the most popular recipes for '''Slime Organ''' will be listed below. {{User:Nightfoxy25_Animations/cook}} == Locations == '''Slime Organ''' is commonly encountered within various types of containers; storage inside Slime Co. bottles; crates encountered within tables and chairs, and oftentimes on the floors; and inside certain objects. However, '''Slime Organ''''s natural form can be encountered all across [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]], growing on corners, specifically on forest and indoor, abandoned levels. It is found more abundantly on [[Level 1]], [[Level 45]], [[Level 118]], [[Level 416]], and [[Level 3]]. Although there are tens of outposts one can encounter on the aforementioned levels that sell, donate, and provide '''Slime Organ''' in all of its forms. == Discovery and Origins == While the origins of '''Slime Organ''' are completely unknown, it is believed that they originated from radiation on certain levels; they may have spread around [[Backrooms Wiki|the Backrooms]], and the original specimens eventually went extinct due to the radiation, causing the object's original level to contain none. Its discovery occurred in 2002, during an exploration on [[Level 789]] by a small group of wanderers. This group would begin selling to the local outposts and begin selling outside of [[Level 789]], forming Slime Co. {{Collapse|num=36|initial=Open Author Info.|final=Close Author Info.|body='''Author''': [[User:Nightfoxy25_Animations|Brigadeiro]] '''Help''': [[User:Indecision13213213213|Indecision]]. *First two images taken by me; *Slime Organ fluid image from https://mthoodhoney.com/products/honey-4-oz-muth-bottles?srsltid=AfmBOorBjAH2Il5uV_c4g0l-PHjc8t2FR0--S9gWvsiXcF5AMdow1knN *Audio: "Bee Theme" by Helen's Piano. {{Audio|align=center|file=Goofy bee theme.mp3|secrettext=So goofy I love Bee Theme!!!}} {{User:Nightfoxy25_Animations/Authorship}}}} [[Category:Biohazard]] [[Category:Food]] [[Category:Objects]] [[Category:Safe]] [[Category:Organisms]]
